chunks.title: Inline & Block Elements in HTML
chunks.text: It is very important to learn the concept of inline and block elements in HTML

chunks.title: Inline & Block Elements in HTML
chunks.text: Inline elements are those which take only the required width

chunks.title: Inline & Block Elements in HTML
chunks.text: and block elements are those which take the entire width of the screen

chunks.title: Inline & Block Elements in HTML
chunks.text: This is also called display property which can be set through CSS

text: It is very important to learn the concept of inline and block elements in HTML Inline elements are those which take only the required width and block elements are those which take the entire width of the screen This is also called display property which can be set through CSS If you are enjoying this video, then please like this video So let's go to the computer screen and see inline and block elements closely Through some very good examples, let's roll the intro So guys, it's time to see inline and block elements If you haven't accessed the playlist or code, then you can access both Because as the videos are being made, the code of the videos is being added to the playlist Now what I will do here is that I will make a folder named video8 I have already added 7 videos here by making a new folder I will make a video8 here and open it in VS Code After opening in VS Code, I will make you guys write index.html and tell you about index and block elements So I typed in an exclamation mark and I could have typed this but I won't because I can't type this I will write here inline and block elements I have told you guys a little bit about this but I will tell you guys in detail about it What are inline elements and why I want to use them So inline elements are those which take only the required width So block elements don't take the required width, they take the entire width Some people are like that, they just take the block elements I will tell you guys the difference And to explain this difference, I will cheat a little bit And the cheating is that I will add CSS You might think that I haven't studied CSS, why am I adding CSS I am adding CSS as much as it is required And here I will just add one thing, I will enter the P here And here I will write, let us say, hey I am a para And I will tell you guys to give the P a background color, let us say blue I will give a nice color here, otherwise you guys will say, at least choose a good color Now you can see here I have given a color to the paragraph And along with that I will add an anchor tag, href is equal to google.com And I will have to add HTTPS HTTPS colon double slash, then it will go to google.com And I will write google And I will give the background color of the anchor tag, background color red But not red, I will choose hover And I can choose any color in this spectrum How did you
